data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized SDK-style replacement:

## Legacy Patterns Identified

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace on `<Project>` | Replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Implicit in SDK |
| 3 | `Configuration`/`Platform` conditions | SDK provides defaults |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 5 | `FileAlignment` | Not needed |
| 6 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Not needed (SDK handles) |
| 7 | `Deterministic` | Default `true` in SDK |
| 8 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK defaults suffice |
| 9 | Explicit `<Reference>` for framework assemblies | Implicit with framework |
| 10 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs` |
| 11 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Replaced by `<PropertyGroup>` metadata |
| 12 | `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Implicit in SDK |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **Old-style project format** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace
2. **Explicit Microsoft.Common.props import** - handled automatically by SDK
3. **Explicit Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** - defaults are provided by SDK
4. **ProjectGuid** - not needed in SDK-style
5. **FileAlignment** - not needed
6. **AutoGenerateBindingRedirects** - not needed for .NET Core/5+


7. **Deterministic** - already true by default in SDK-style
8. **Debug/Release PropertyGroups** - SDK provides sensible defaults
9. **Explicit framework assembly references** (System, System.Core, etc.) - implicit in SDK-style
10. **Explicit Compile ItemGroup** - SDK-style uses globbing (auto-includes *.cs)
11. **AssemblyInfo.cs** - can be replaced with project properties
12. **Microsoft.CSharp.targets import** - replaced by SDK attribute
13. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** - can be modernized to `net472` or upgraded to `net8.0`

I'll go ahead and apply these changes to bring the project file up to date.

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines**. Here's what was done:

**Changes made:**
1. Converted to SDK-style (`Microsoft.NET.Sdk`) — eliminates all imports/targets
2. Replaced `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `TargetFramework net472`
3. Removed all Debug/Release property groups (SDK defaults handle these)
4. Removed explicit `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `*.cs`)
5. Removed framework `<Reference>` items (implicit with `net472`)
6. Moved assembly metadata from `AssemblyInfo.cs` into `<PropertyGroup>`
7. Deleted `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` (now generated automatically by SDK)
8. Removed `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `OutputType` (all defaults)

The `OutputType` defaults to `Library`, so it's omitted. If you later want to target .NET 8+, just change `<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>`.
